title: Nations Have the Right to Kill
note: Restricted metadata only until source access is clarified.

title: Proclamation to the German Nation
note: Radio broadcast, 1 February 1933. Reproduced in Baynes, Norman H., ed., The Speeches of Adolf Hitler, April 1922–August 1939, Vol. 1 (London: Oxford University Press, 1942). Passage text requires verification against Baynes or equivalent scholarly edition before advancing beyond source-review.

title: War Powers Resolution
note: Public Law 93-148; 87 Stat. 555; codified at 50 U.S.C. §§ 1541–1548. Public domain US federal law.

title: Address to the Nation on Energy and National Goals ("Crisis of Confidence")
note: Public domain US government document. Archived at the Jimmy Carter Presidential Library and the Miller Center, University of Virginia.

title: Resignation Address
note: Broadcast on Soviet state television, 25 December 1991. English translation reproduced in Gorbachev, Mikhail, Memoirs (New York: Doubleday, 1996) and widely in press archives. Passage text requires verification against a scholarly edition.

title: Alma-Ata Declaration

author.literal: Commonwealth of Independent States founding states

note: Signed 21 December 1991 by eleven former Soviet republics. Public domain. Reproduced in International Legal Materials 31:1 (1992), p. 148.

title: Imperial Rescript on Education (Kyōiku Chokugo)
note: Public domain. English translation published by the Japanese Ministry of Education. Widely reproduced in Tsunoda, Ryusaku, Wm. Theodore de Bary, and Donald Keene, eds., Sources of Japanese Tradition, Vol. 2 (New York: Columbia University Press, 1958).

title: Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors (Gunjin Chokuyu)
note: Public domain. English translation in McLaren, W. W., ed., Japanese Government Documents, Transactions of the Asiatic Society of Japan 42 (1914).

title: Regierungserklärung (First Government Declaration)
note: Delivered to the Bundestag, 20 September 1949. Public domain. German text in Verhandlungen des Deutschen Bundestages, 1. Wahlperiode, 5. Sitzung. English translation in Adenauer, Konrad, Memoirs 1945–53 (London: Weidenfeld & Nicolson, 1966). Passage text requires verification against cited edition.

title: Basic Law for the Federal Republic of Germany (Grundgesetz)
note: Public domain. Official English translation published by the Deutscher Bundestag (2012 ed.). Art. 1(1): human dignity inviolable. Art. 79(3): eternity clause placing Art. 1 beyond amendment.

title: Program of the National Socialist German Workers' Party (25 Points)
note: Public domain. Announced 24 February 1920, Hofbräuhaus, Munich. Reproduced in Stackelberg, Roderick, and Sally A. Winkle, eds., The Nazi Germany Sourcebook (London: Routledge, 2002), pp. 27–31.
